canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, CIFAR-100 is consistently characterized as an image dataset with 100 object classes used for evaluation. Its most prevalent application is to measure and benchmark model performance on the task of `image classification`. Several papers explicitly frame it as a more complex or challenging vision task compared to CIFAR-10, sometimes describing its 100 classes as "fine-grained". While its primary use is for standard image classification, a smaller set of studies employs it to assess more specific behaviors. For instance, some papers use it to evaluate `generalization`, particularly for prompt-based methods, while others use it for `zero-shot image classification` experiments. The dataset also serves as a testbed for a variety of other evaluations, including text-conditioned clustering, automated accuracy prediction under data shifts, neural compression techniques, and `memory efficiency`.

definitions.text: An image dataset with 100 object classes, used to evaluate the proposed text-conditioned clustering method in a more complex setting compared to classical methods.
definitions.paper: Bootstrapping Variational Information Pursuit with Large Language and Vision Models for Interpretable Image Classification

definitions.text: An image classification dataset similar to CIFAR10 but with 100 fine-grained classes, presenting a more challenging vision task.
definitions.paper: Dynamic Sparse Training of Diagonally Sparse Networks

relationships.paper: NOLA: Compressing LoRA using Linear Combination of Random Basis
relationships.evidence: We compare MCNC on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky et al., 2009) with both pruning methods as well as PRANC (Nooralinejad et al., 2023) and NOLA (Koohpayegani et al., 2024) under extreme compression rates. (Section 4.1)

relationships.paper: Understanding prompt engineering may not require rethinking generalization
relationships.evidence: In this section, we evaluate the generalization of discrete prompts generated by Greedy on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, ImageNet as well as domain generalization datasets fMoW (Christie et al., 2018) and OfficeHome (Venkateswara et al., 2017) (Section 5)

relationships.paper: From Weight-Based to State-Based Fine-Tuning: Further Memory Reduction on LoRA with Parallel Control
relationships.evidence: We begin our evaluation with a toy example using the CIFAR-100 dataset... The parallel control approach proves to be an effective fine-tuning method, offering lower memory consumption and reduced training time while maintaining competitive performance. (Section 5.1)
